name: Sahel Belt
climate: Arid to semi-arid; hot dry season and short wet season; rainfall highly variable; Saharan influence in the north, savanna in the south

neighbors.name: ECOWAS
neighbors.relevance: Most Sahel states are ECOWAS members; Mali, Niger, and Burkina Faso suspended after coups

neighbors.name: Horn of Africa
neighbors.relevance: Eastern neighbor via Sudan and Chad; shared conflict dynamics and displacement corridors

neighbors.name: African Union
neighbors.relevance: AU mediates Sahel conflicts and coordinates counter-terrorism operations

neighbors.name: Mediterranean
neighbors.relevance: Sahel is the origin of major migration routes north through Libya and Algeria to Mediterranean

neighbors.name: Red Sea
neighbors.relevance: Sudan connects the Sahel to the Red Sea coast; gold and commodity export route

links.description: Mali, Niger, and Burkina Faso left ECOWAS after military coups; regional bloc fragmentation
Instability flows between regions via Sudan; shared food insecurity and displacement crises
AU and UN peacekeeping operations across Mali, Chad, and Sudan
EU is the largest aid donor and migration management partner for Sahel states
Chinese mining investments in Niger uranium, Malian gold, and Chadian oil
Trans-Saharan migration and trade routes connect Sahel to North African Mediterranean ports
Nigeria is both a Sahel-bordering state and OPEC member; oil revenue and regional security intertwined
